title: Fermented Mexican Cabbage Quesadilla
description: A crunchy and flavorful twist on traditional Mexican quesadillas, featuring fermented cabbage as the main ingredient.
instructions: In a blender or food processor, blend the fermentation brine, water, and active cultures until smooth and well combined.
Add the blended mixture to the cabbage and mix until the cabbage is well coated.
Transfer the cabbage mixture to a glass container with a wide mouth, such as a Mason jar or a crock.
Cover the container with a lid or plastic wrap and let it ferment at room temperature (about 70-75�02F) for 3-5 days, or until it reaches the desired level of sourness.
After 3-5 days, remove the cabbage from the fermentation container and discard the liquid.
In a large skillet, heat the olive oil over medium heat.
Add the shredded cabbage and cook until softened and caramelized, about 10-15 minutes.
In a separate pan, melt the butter or heat the oil over medium heat.
Add the flour tortillas and cook for 1-2 minutes on each side, until they are lightly browned and crispy.
In a separate bowl, mix together the shredded vegan cheese, chopped cilantro, and a pinch of salt and pepper.
To assemble the quesadillas, place a cooked tortilla on a flat surface and sprinkle a quarter of the cheese mixture onto half of the tortilla.
Add a few pieces of the cooked cabbage to the cheese mixture, followed by a sprinkle of salt and pepper.
Fold the tortilla in half to enclose the filling and press gently to seal.
Cook the quesadilla in the pan with the butter or oil for 2-3 minutes on each side, until it is crispy and golden brown.
Repeat with the remaining tortillas and filling.
Cut the quesadillas into wedges and serve hot with your favorite toppings.
Note: You can also add other toppings such as diced tomatoes, shredded lettuce, diced avocado, or sliced black olives to make the quesadillas more flavorful and filling.
Also, you can use different types of cheese such as cheddar, mozzarella, or feta instead of the vegan cheese, but be aware that they may melt differently and change the texture of the quesadillas.
You can also add some heat to the quesadillas by sprinkling some cayenne pepper or red pepper flakes on top of the cheese mixture before folding the tortilla.
